Strip_Summary: First-person POV from patrol car interior. Rural road at night. Suspect visible in back seat.
Stickman cop by patrol car on dark road. Putting suspect in back. Not calling it in.
Radio on belt untouched. Stickman standing by car on empty road. Hand away from radio.
Bold white text on dark: "Not because you forgot. Because you decided not to."
Bold white text on black: "They were never here."
Young stickman in fresh uniform walking precinct hallway. Night shift. Burnt coffee smell.
Everything new. Badge catching light when stickman moves. Precinct night shift glow.
Belt pulling at hips. Not used to weight yet. Young cop adjusting gear.
Stickman watching cop walk someone past booking desk, past cameras. Through unlabeled door.
Door with no label. Cop does not come back. Neither does person. Hallway silent.
Waiting for someone to say something. No one does. Young stickman standing alone.
Holding empty cup. Still holding it. Precinct quiet around him.
Young stickman facing senior cop near hallway. Asking about the door. No answer, just a look.
Conversation over before finished asking. Kind of look that ends things.
Young stickman nodding. Understanding nothing. Cool blue wash.
Precinct corridor near unlabeled door. Everyone knows. No one says it in front of captain.
Break room: stickman sitting. TV on wall. Normal silence. No one mentions anything.
Stickman rinsing cup at sink. TV playing game. Another cop watching. Ordinary Tuesday.
Stickman placing cup back. Like any other Tuesday. Nothing mentioned.
Bold white text on dark grey: "Silence is the first disappearance."
Stickman in plainclothes at 3AM. Walking past booking desk. Tired. Turning left.
Past cameras. Reaching for cold door handle on unlabeled door. Dark hallway.
Door handle cold. Door with no label. Room with no camera. Stickman entering.
Interior: chair, table, flickering fluorescent. No window. Bare room.
No vent. Air does not move in here. Still room. Empty.
Stickman closing heavy door. Lock clicking different. Heavier. Alone in hallway.
Stickman at closed door asking one question. Faceless figure saying no. Door closing.
Patrol car at night during drive. Phone rings three times, goes quiet. Battery removed.
Battery warm in jacket pocket against ribs. Feeling it through lining. Night highway.
Wallet in glove box. Keys in cup holder. Belongings of someone who was never here.
Gas station dumpster at night. Stickman slowing car, not stopping. Everything tossed.
Next morning: stickman checking room. Empty. Checking twice. No one asks.
Desk sergeant typing. Does not look up. Stickman walking past.
Friend calls precinct two days later. Stickman hearing sergeant across room.
Sergeant: no one by that name. Not looking up. Said it before. Routine denial.
True. Never brought them in. Brought them somewhere else. Stickman at desk.
Stickman leaning back in chair, exhaling. Whole precinct feels normal. Fluorescent humming.
Fluorescent light above desk. First time. First time the system held.
Bold text on dark: "Not because it worked. Because there was nothing to hold."
Stickman lying in bed, morning light. Badge on nightstand. Six hours straight. Best sleep.
Computer terminal at night. Booking system empty. Screen glow. Three checks. Same result.
Bold white text on black: "They were never here."
Second time. Not easier, clearer. Known route. Third door, no label. Handle warm.
Bold text: "You are not testing the system anymore. You are using it."
Patrol car at night. Stickman choosing. Not paperwork, not late. Because he can.
Testing who disappears. Phone ringing person. Job mention person. Three faceless figures.
Someone waiting at home. Learning patterns. Not who breaks. Who gets missed.
Stickman in patrol car tapping dashboard once. Dark lot. Controlled stillness.
Bold text: "You are not choosing people. You are choosing how much they exist."
One answers yes. My wife. Stickman pausing. Patrol car interior at night.
Left: stickman fingerprinted at booking desk. Right: empty room door, no record.
Normal way. Watching process like never seen. All procedure for one who said yes.
Bold text: "You are choosing who gets rights."
Lawyer calls. Sergeant at desk says no record. Client in room. Stickman overhearing.
Lawyer voice through receiver. Asking again. Louder. Stickman adjusting belt.
Stickman rigid, hand on belt. Thirty feet from phone. Desaturated grey vignette.
Lawyer calls next day. Same answer. Same silence after.
Client stops asking for phone. Stops asking for anything. Room going quiet.
Water cup placed through cracked door. Untouched next morning. Still there. Full.
Walking past unlabeled door. Air near door still. Always still.
Voice inside asking for phone call. Recognizing the voice. Not stopping.
Feet not slowing down. Keep walking. Hallway stretching.
Vending machine end of hall. Out of everything except water. Taking the water.
How many right now. Checking like weather. A number. Not names. Never names.
Number in head like checking account balance. Something monitored. Not talked about.
Adjusting count when things change. Not writing it down. Quiet routine.
Checking room before shift. Not empty. Should be. Stickman at open door.
Did not put anyone there. Or does not remember. Stickman uncertain at door.
Either forgot. Or someone else using room. Someone who does not work here.
Break room. Too-hot coffee. Drinking anyway. Unbothered. Normal routine.
Counting this week. One more than remembered. Recount. Same number.
Pen cap on floor near door. Not picking it up. Room operating independently.
Stickman alone near door. Room does not wait. Fills on its own. Cool blue dread wash.
Driving home on back streets. No cameras. Route 9. Choosing convenience now.
Room interior. Faceless figure stopped asking about phone. Asking when to leave.
No answer. The question assumes they arrived. Stickman at door, silent.
Sound one night from other side of precinct. Door opening. Heavy click.
Stickman alone in corridor. Did not open it. Grey vignette. Dread.
Late night. Standing near booking desk. Precinct mostly empty.
Sandwich wrapper on counter. Clock says 11:47. Empty precinct at night.
Jaw tight. Not sure when that started. Desaturated grey. Pre-dread.
Phone rings. Knowing before hearing name. Before sergeant picks up. Already knowing.
First-person POV: phone receiver. Small child voice coming through. Dark precinct.
Sergeant picking up. No one by that name tonight. Stickman frozen thirty feet away.
Child asks again. Says name slower this time. Same answer. Stickman hearing.
Almost stepping forward. Foot moves. Weight shifts. Grey wash. Crisis.
Weight shifting. Throat tightening, swallowing. Then gone. Cold grey wash.
Stopping. Stepping forward means unwinding every absence ever made. Standing still.
Bold text on black: "Every left turn. Every closed door. Every name that was never entered."
Thirty feet. One wall. Unlabeled door. Air on this side still. Always still.
Child hangs up. Click. Dial tone. Dark precinct.
Hands still. Did not move. They were never here. Grey to black vignette.
System works. Database empty. Picking up sandwich wrapper. Throwing away. Mundane.
Stickman standing alone in empty precinct. Night. Staying. Grey fading to black.
New cop in fresh uniform. Radio still on. Sees left turn. Does not ask.
Stickman remembering not asking. Remembering the cup. Already finished it.
Coffee machine. New cop says "What person?" Own words from another mouth.
Tall stickman watching words leave new cop mouth. Warm amber bittersweet glow.
New cop holding unlabeled door. Holds it like done a hundred times. Not looking inside.
Lock clicks behind them. Same sound. Stickman hearing from distance.
Two weeks later. New cop leads someone to room alone. Was not there when it happened.
Nobody told them to. Or maybe somebody did. Does not know anymore.
Tallest stickman trying to remember being new. Cannot find that version. Amber fade.
Hearing new cop ask the question. Same words. Same tone. Through hallway door.
Tallest stickman alone in corridor. Does not remember teaching. Does not remember learning.
Bold text on black: "They were never here."
Checking system. No record. Staring at screen. Cursor blinking.
Cannot tell if erased or never existed. Closing tab. Cursor gone. Expression empty. Grey.
Phone on desk. Rang three times. Finding memory. Deleting it. Night precinct.
Bold text: "Names create records. Records create people."
Patrol car at night. Same route. Same left turn. New faceless person in back.
Stickman hand on warm handle. Same door, no label. Not asking their name.
Bold text: "They were never here." Smaller: "You do not remember if you were."
